data.body:
PASS-1 review (criterion G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection). One coaching finding; the rest of G6 is affirmed.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this plan passes):
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: ISF targets the named problem directly — silent descoping of an EXTERNALLY-expressed requirement (the gate's plan-internal blind spot), not a proxy. The "Why" frames it precisely.
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS (edge/empty/failure handled): gating + fallbacks are sound. Fires ONLY when a session log is linked (no fabricated baseline when absent); oversized log -> summarize the LOG ONLY (never the plan) + RECORD + carry reduced confidence (summarize_for_isf + pass1_isf's summarized/_reduced_confidence tag, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:292-336).
- APPROACH SELECTION (defensible; alternatives discarded): I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) an AGENTIC/tool-using ISF that fetches the log on demand, and (b) folding intent-fidelity into the existing E3 (plan-vs-title) criterion rather than a dedicated child. The plan explicitly forecloses (a) ("NOT agent/tool-using") and the descriptor separates ISF from E3; the chosen single-turn-fed-context approach is at-least-as-good on determinism, blast-radius, and testability. PASS — alternatives discarded.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: "has distinct mechanics, so it gets its own child" (own-child rationale) + non-agentic justified for determinism.

FINDING (G6 — mechanism/data-flow completeness):
- [ ] Make the "ticket graph" feed concrete in the data-flow / AC #1, or drop it from the descriptor. The What bullet 1 and AC #1 both state ISF is "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved TICKET GRAPH", and the criterion's check is "Each expressed requirement is honored by the plan + ticket graph, or descoped WITH a stated rationale" (criteria_routing.json:1161). But the actual mechanism feeds ONLY the plan ({{plan}} var) + the session-log text as instructions — no pre-resolved ticket-graph blob reaches the model (pass1_isf, src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py:304-318 passes only session_log_text + plan; _resolve_system at :171-180 renders only the {{plan}} var). As written, step (2)'s "honored by the plan + ticket graph" cannot actually be evaluated, so a requirement satisfied by a SIBLING/CHILD ticket (not the plan body) would be mis-flagged as a silent drop — a false-fire that validation AC #3 ("no false-fire on honored/justified-descope plans") does not explicitly cover. Specify in AC #1 HOW the ticket graph is rendered into the ISF input (e.g. a resolved-graph blob in instructions), or remove "ticket graph" from the descriptor if the plan body alone is the intended comparison target.
  location: ticket 681b — "What" bullet 1 + Acceptance Criteria #1
  criteria: [G6]
  evidence: What bullet "FED the linked session log + the pre-resolved ticket graph"; AC #1 "fed session log + ticket graph"; criteria_routing.json:1161 check "honored by the plan + ticket graph"; ABSENCE in pass1_isf data-flow (passes.py:304-318 passes only session_log_text + {{plan}}; no ticket-graph blob; _resolve_system at :171-180 renders only {{plan}}).
